segments.text:
My spirit is consumed. My days are extinct
and the grave is ready for me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Surely there are mockers with me. My eye dwells on their provocation.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “Now give a pledge. Be collateral for me with yourself. Who is there who will strike hands with me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For you have hidden their heart from understanding, therefore you will not exalt them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He who denounces his friends for plunder, even the eyes of his children will fail.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “But he has made me a byword of the people. They spit in my face.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My eye also is dim by reason of sorrow. All my members are as a shadow.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Upright men will be astonished at this. The innocent will stir himself up against the godless.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yet the righteous will hold to his way. He who has clean hands will grow stronger and stronger.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But as for you all, come back. I will not find a wise man among you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
My days are past. My plans are broken off, 
as are the thoughts of my heart.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They change the night into day, saying ‘The light is near’ in the presence of darkness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I look for Sheol as my house, if I have spread my couch in the darkness,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have said to corruption, ‘You are my father,’and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ and ‘My sister,’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: where then is my hope? As for my hope, who will see it?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Shall it go down with me to the gates of Sheol,  or descend together into the dust?
segments.speaker: Job
